Q2 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

At equinox days, the declination angle is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The declination angle changes from a maximum value of +23.45° on June 21 to a minimum
of –23.45° on December 22. The declination is zero on two equinox days i.e. March 22 and
September 22.

Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

During winter, the optimum tilt angle of collectors is

 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
During winter, the tilt angle is kept 10 - 15° more than the latitude of the location while in
summer it should be 10 - 15° less than the latitude.

Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. The highest value of power coefficient can be obtained with the propeller type rotors.
2. Multiblade and savonious type rotors have a high value of tip speed ratio varying from 3 to 7.
3. Propeller and Darrieus type rotors have a low value of tip speed ratio around unity.
4. Propeller rotor curves are not sharp while for other rotors the rise and fall of power coefficient is quite fast around the maximum value.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Multiblade and savonius type rotors have low value of tip speed ratio (λ) around unity while the
propeller and Darrieus type rotors have a high value of λ varying from 3 to 7. As the tip speed
ratio increases, the number of blades decreases.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q11 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

What is the favourable ratio of carbon to nitrogen for biogas production?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Carbon is required for energy while nitrogen for building cell protein. The consumption of carbon
is 30 to 35 times faster than that of nitrogen. A favourable ratio of C : N can be taken as 30 : 1. Any
deviation from this ratio lowers the biogas production.

Q12 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which one of the following is NOT correct for piezoelectric accelerometers?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
High frequency response (10 Hz to 50 kHz). Unsuitable for applications where the input frequency
is lower than 10 Hz.

Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding sensor characteristics:
1. Range is the difference between the largest and the smallest reading.
2. Span represents the algebric difference between the upper and lower range values of the sensor.
3. The ratio of the magnitude of input signal to output signal is called sensitivity.
4. The maximum differences in output at any measured value within the specified range when
approaching the point first with increasing and then with decreasing input is known as Hysteresis.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Sensitivity : The ratio of the magnitude of output signal to the input signal or response of
measuring system to the quantity being measured is called sensitivity. It is represented by the
slope of the calibration curve if the ordinates are expressed in actual units.

Q16 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A load cell is essentially a

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Load cells are elastic devices that can be used for measurement of force through indirect methods.
When the combination of the strain guage-elastic member is used for weighing, it is called a “load
cell”.

Q17 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A two-port resistive network, used to reduce the signal level by a given amount is known as

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
In a number of applications, it is necessary to introduce a specified loss between the source and a matched load without altering the impedance relationship. Attenuators can be used for this purpose. An attenuator is a two-port resistive network and is used to reduce the signal level by a given amount.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Quantizing is defined as the transformation of a continuous analog input into a set of discrete output states.

Q37 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): A robot programming language serves as an interface between the user and the robot manipulator.
Statement (II): In on-line programming, the robot is not tied-up and can continue doing its task, that is, there is no loss of production.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
In on-line programming, the manipulator executes the command as soon as it is entered and the programmer can verify whether the robot executes the desired task. Any discrepancy is, therefore, corrected immediately. Statement II is correct for offline programming.

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which one of the following is the main advantage of air vessel when fitted on delivery side of reciprocating pump?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Advantage of air vessel on delivery side
(i) Almost constant delivery discharge is obtained.
(ii) Reduction in friction loss and hence saving in power.

Q50 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): The operation of compressor on the left of the peak of the performance curve is injurious to the machine.
Statement (II): Surging of the compressor leads to vibration of the entire machine which ultimately lead to mechanical failure.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Surge points on each curve corresponding to different speeds can be located and a surge line drawn. The stable range of operation of the compressor is on the right hand side of this line.

h1 = 3300 kJ/kg, h2 = 2766 kJ/kg, h2′ = 2842 kJ/kg, h3 = 3417 kJ/kg, h4 = 2367 kJ/kg

(d)

Q67 Not checked

Correct answer: D
Solution

(ii) Leakage losses from the end seals.

Q70 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding exhaust emissions:
1. The main non-exhaust is the unburnt hydrocarbons from fuel tank and crankcase blowby.
2. CO emissions will be very high at all equivalence ratio.
3. NOx emission will steadily increase as equivalence ratio increase.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• CO emissions will be very low at all equivalence ratio since excess air is always available.
• NOx
emission will steadily increase as equivalence ratio increase due to increasing fraction of
cylinder contents due to increasing fraction of cylinder contents being burnt gases close to
stoichiometric during combustion, and also due to higher peak temperatures and pressures.

Q71 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): Fuel-air cycle analysis suggests that the thermal efficiency will deteriorate as the mixture supplied to an engine is enriched.
Statement (II): Losses due to variable specific heats and dissociation result in decrease of thermal efficiency.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Fuel-air cycle analysis suggests that the thermal efficiency will deteriorate as the mixture supplied
to an engine is enriched. This is explained by the increasing losses due to variable specific heats
and dissociation as the mixture strength approaches chemically correct values. This is because,
the gas temperature goes up after combustion as the mixture strength approaches chemically
correct values. Enrichment beyond the chemically correct ratio will lead to incomplete combustion
and loss in thermal efficiency. Therefore, it will appear that thermal efficiency will increase as the
mixture is made leaner.

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Q74 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

Consider the following statements regarding battery ignition system:
1. The primary winding consists of many turns of fine copper wire while the secondary winding consists of few turns of heavy copper wire.
2. The primary voltage decreases as the engine speed increases.
3. The rapid collapse and reversal of the magnetic field in the core induce a very high voltage in the secondary winding.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• The ignition coil of two coils of wire, one wound around the other, insulated from each other;
the primary winding consists of few turns of heavy copper wire and the secondary winding
consists of many turns of fine copper wire.
• Statement 2 is correct and it occurs due to limitations in the current switching capability of the
breaker system.

Q81 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding refrigerants:
1. Refrigerants that are not miscible with oil pose problems in case of hermetically sealed compressors.
2. The phenomenon of moisture choking occurs in compressor.
3. In halide torch method of leak detection, the flame colour changes from light blue to bluish green in presence of ammonia.
Which of the above statements are INCORRECT?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
If more water is present than can be dissolved by the refrigerant, then there is danger of ice
formation and consequent choking in the expansion valve or capillary tube used for throttling.
This phenomenon is called moisture choking.

Q82 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following are influenced or changed when a flash chamber is used in a vapour compression refrigeration system?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
A flash chamber is an insulated container and it separates the liquid and vapour refrigerant coming out of expansion valve due to centrifugal effect. Due to this, the mass of the refrigerant passing through the evaporator reduces. This results in reduction in the mass of refrigerant flowing through the evaporator and hence the reduction in the size of evaporator.

Q83 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q84 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): The performance of a centrifugal compressor is generally expressed in terms of adiabatic efficiency rather than isothermal efficiency.
Statement (II): The minimum work of compression, with cooling, is isothermal work.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• The minimum work of compression, with cooling, is isothermal work. Often isothermal efficiency is, therefore, used to express the performance of reciprocating compressors which are invariably cooled.
• The minimum of compression, without cooling, is isentropic work. Adiabatic efficiency is,
therefore, used to express the performance of centrifugal compressors in which it is not possible to arrange cooling during compression.

Q85 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the use of expansion devices in a refrigeration system:
1. Automatic expansion valves find applications where precise control of evaporator temperature is needed.
2. Thermostatic expansion valves keep the evaporator always full of the refrigerant.
3. An external-equilizer connection helps in transmitting the pressure at the outlet of evaporator to inside of the bellows.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• An automatic-expansion valve is not suitable for varying-load requirement. It is, however,
used in applications such as milk coolers where precise control of the evaporator temperature
is needed and where the cooling load is more or less constant.
• A thermostatic expansion valve keeps the evaporators always full of the refrigerant, regardless
of the changes in the cooling load, by maintaining a constant degree of superheat of the
suction vapour.
• An external-equalizer connection transmits the pressure at the outlet of the evaporator to the
inside of the bellows of the expansion valve. At the same time, there is no connection between
the evaporator inlet and inside of the bellows. Thus, the pressure acting inside the bellows is
always equal to the pressure at the evaporator outlet, irrespective of the extent of pressure
drop in the evaporator tubing.

Q86 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding recovery, grain growth and recrystallization:
1. The driving force for recovery, grain growth and recrystallization is stored energy of cold work.
2. Grain growth occurs only after recovery and recrystallization.
3. At a particular temperatures, grain growth occurs slowly while recrystallization occurs at substantially high speeds.
Which of the above statements are INCORRECT?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The driving force for grain growth is reduction in grain boundary region while for recovery
and recrystallization, it is stored energy of cold work.
• Grain growth follows complete crystallization if the material is left at elevated temperatures.
However, grain growth does not need to be preceded by recovery and recrystallization; it
may occur in all polycrystalline materials.

Not attempted

In corrosion of metals by a gas like oxygen, the surface layer or film grows with time

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The surface layer or film grows with time in following fashions:
• linear (in case of porous films)
• parabolic (in case of non porous adherent films)
• logarithmic
These three modes are known as laws of oxidation.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
For compressive stresses, there is no stress amplification associated with any existent flows. For this reason, brittle ceramics displace much higher strengths in compression than in tension.

Q101 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following changes in specimen affect the results of a rotating beam test?
1. Surface condition
2. Stress state
3. Temperature
4. Size
Select the correct answer using codes given below:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Rotating beam test is a laboratory method used to determine endurance limit. The endurance
limit of a mechanical or structural member deviates from the experimentally determined values.
These deviations are due to :
• Material : Composition, basis of failure, variability.
• Manufacturing : Method, heat treatment, surface condition, stress concentration.
• Environment : Corrosion, temperature, stress state, relaxation times.
• Design : Size, shape, life, speed, etc.

Q106 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding mechanical brakes:
1. The brake should be self-locking in general applications.
2. The use of double block or shoe brakes is preferred over single block or shoe brake.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• The brake should be self energizing and not self locking in general applications. The selflocking brake is used only in back-stop applications.
• When a single block or shoe brake is applied to a rolling wheel, an additional load is thrown
on shaft bearings due to heavy normal force and produces bending of the shaft. This drawback
is overcome by double block or shoe brakes.

Q107 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding energy interaction in a thermodynamic system:
1. Work is a higher grade of energy as compared to heat.
2. Kinetic and potential energies are considered to be organised form of energy.
3. Internal energy cannot be readily converted into work.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• The kinetic energy and potential energy are also called organized form of energy that can be readily converted into work.
• Internal energy is due to random motions or disorganised motions of molecules. The internal
energy cannot be readily converted into work.

Q111 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): It takes longer to cook at higher altitude regions.
Statement (II): The weather is generally colder at higher altitude regions.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The atmospheric pressure, and thus the boiling temperature of water, decreases with elevation.
Therefore, it takes longer to cook at higher altitudes than it does at sea level. For each 1000 m
increase in elevation, the boiling temperature drops by a little over 3°C.
Note that the atmospheric pressure at a location, and thus the boiling temperature, changes
slightly with the weather conditions. But the corresponding change in the boiling temperature is
no more than about 1°C.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q136 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following can be categorized as a procurement cost?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Procurement costs : This includes the cost of order preparation, tender placement, cost of postages, telephone costs, receiving costs, set up cost, etc.
Carrying costs : This represents the cost of maintaining inventories in the plant. It includes the cost of insurance, security, warehouse rent, taxes, interest on capital engaged, spoilage, breakage, etc.
Stockout costs : This represents the cost of loss of demand due to shortage in supplies. This
includes cost of loss of profit, loss of customer, loss of goodwill, penalty, etc.

Q141 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding insulation of pipes and electrical wires :
1. Thickness of insulation for pipes is taken higher than critical thickness.
2. Thickness of insulation for wires is kept lower than critical thickness.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Statement 1 is correct. This is so because any insulation added will only decrease the heat loss from the pipe.
• Statement 2 is correct. This is so that the insulation added increases the heat loss from the electrical wires.

Q142 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. The revolute (R) and the prismatic (P) pairs are the only lower pairs usable in a planar mechanism.
2. The slider joint is preferred over pin joint for low wear and long life.
3. A ball-and-socket joint is a 3 DOF joint.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The revolute (R) and the prismatic (P) pairs are the only lower pairs usable in a planar mechanism. The screw (H), cylindric (C), spherical (S) and flat (F) lower pairs are all combinations of the revolute and/or prismatic pairs and are used in spatial (3D) mechanisms.
• The main practical advantage of lower pairs over higher pairs is their better ability to trap lubricant between their enveloping surfaces. The lubricant is more easily squeezed out of a higher pair, non enveloping joint. As a result, the pin joint is preferred for low wear and long life over slider joint.

Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: B

(b)

Q147 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding gears and gear trains:
1. The mechanical advantage of a gearset is the reciprocal of velocity ratio.
2. With an involute tooth form, centre distance errors do not affect the velocity ratio.
3. Backlash is not a problem in a gearset which runs with a non-reversing torque.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• The torque ratio is the reciprocal of the velocity ratio; thus a gearset is essentially a device to exchange torque for velocity or vice versa. Since there are no applied forces as, in a linkage, but only applied torque on the gears, the mechanical advantage of a gearset is equal to its torque ratio.
• Backlash is defined as the clearance between mating teeth measured along the circumference of the pitch circle. As long as the gearset is run with a nonreversing torque, backlash should not be a problem. But, whenever torque changes sign, the teeth will move from contact on one side to the other.
